Name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Adaptive Precision Allocation via Hessian Spectral Geometry
Short Hypothesis: The curvature of the loss landscape, as quantified by the Hessian spectrum, provides a deterministic proxy for precision sensitivity in neural network inference, enabling training-free adaptive precision allocation that optimizes the throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier.
Related Work: Recent work in precision routing, such as Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR), has explored activation covariance geometry for precision allocation. However, these approaches focus on activation statistics rather than loss landscape curvature, which is a more direct measure of precision sensitivity. Our proposal distinguishes itself by leveraging Hessian spectral geometry, providing a more theoretically grounded and empirically precise mechanism for precision allocation.
Abstract: We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a training-free framework for adaptive precision allocation in neural network inference. CAPR leverages the curvature of the loss landscape, quantified by the Hessian spectrum, to determine the optimal precision for each layer. By computing the Hessian spectrum on-the-fly using randomized SVD, CAPR maps curvature diagnostics to a precision-switching policy that enforces strict latency budgets. We theoretically ground CAPR with PAC-Bayes bounds that link Hessian conditioning to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, CAPR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. CAPR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Hessian Spectrum Profiling - Profile the Hessian spectrum of various layers in a neural network using randomized SVD. Evaluate the correlation between Hessian eigenvalues and precision sensitivity.

Experiment 2: Precision Allocation Policy - Develop and test a precision allocation policy based on Hessian spectral geometry. Compare the performance of CAPR against SQR and static precision baselines.
Experiment 3: Throughput-Accuracy Pareto Frontier - Measure the throughput-accuracy trade-offs of CAPR on WILDS benchmarks. Use PAC-Bayes bounds to validate the generalization stability of CAPR.

Experiment 4: Ablation Studies - Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of Hessian spectral geometry on precision allocation. Evaluate the robustness of CAPR to different initialization seeds and input distributions.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Risk 1: Computational Overhead - The on-the-fly computation of the Hessian spectrum may introduce significant computational overhead, potentially offsetting the benefits of adaptive precision allocation.
Risk 2: Hessian Estimation Accuracy - The accuracy of the Hessian spectrum estimation using randomized SVD may be limited, particularly for deep networks with high-dimensional parameter spaces.
Risk 3: Generalization to Different Architectures - The effectiveness of CAPR may vary across different neural network architectures, requiring architecture-specific tuning.
Risk 4: Latency Budget Enforcement - Enforcing strict latency budgets while maintaining high accuracy may be challenging, particularly for real-time applications.
